differential_diagnoses: Aortic dissection (complicated by Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia)
Vertebral osteomyelitis or discitis (secondary to S. aureus bacteremia)
next_steps: Perform transesophageal echocardiography to evaluate for valvular vegetations and aortic root involvement
Perform CT angiography of the aorta with contrast to evaluate for mycotic aneurysm or dissection given the mural thickening and fat stranding at the aortic hiatus
MRI of the thoracic spine to rule out spondylodiscitis and epidural abscess
Serial blood cultures to confirm clearance of Staphylococcus aureus
Infectious disease consultation for optimized long-term antibiotic therapy

differential_diagnoses: Aortic mycotic aneurysm with contained rupture or expansion
Infective endocarditis with systemic septic embolization
Thoracic spondylodiscitis or epidural abscess (S. aureus)
Septic pulmonary emboli from right-sided endocarditis or IV catheter site
Type 2 myocardial infarction secondary to severe sepsis and tachycardia
next_steps: Perform urgent transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to assess for valvular vegetations and aortic root involvement
Obtain urgent CT angiography of the chest and abdomen with focus on the previously noted aortic mural thickening and fat stranding to evaluate for progression or rupture
Obtain MRI of the thoracic spine to rule out epidural abscess or discitis involving the T8-T9 levels
Continue aggressive intravenous anti-staphylococcal therapy tailored to susceptibility results
Immediate vascular and cardiothoracic surgery consultation
Strict blood pressure control (e.g., target systolic BP < 120 mmHg) to mitigate risk of aortic rupture
Serial blood cultures to confirm clearance of Staphylococcus aureus
Obtain inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP) to assess systemic inflammatory response
